claim_status: Build-information readiness gap ledger only; no complete documentation claim, no all-information-ready-for-building claim, no proposed task approval, no TASK-000011 acceptance, no readiness promotion, no broad M1 implementation claim, no developer preview claim, no beta claim, no stable claim, no production claim, no Chrome-class claim, no performance claim, no compatibility claim, no security claim, no accessibility claim, no release readiness claim, and no daily-driver claim.

information_classes.name: Pinned compiler, SDK, linker, and toolchain reproduction
required_information: Versioned platform compiler, SDK, linker, Rust, Cargo, Git, shell, and environment manifests tied to the reference build.
Independent fresh-host or owner-approved clean-VM reproduction beyond reference-host evidence.
missing_information: Platform compiler, SDK, and linker manifests retained with the independent run record.
Owner-reviewed acceptance or rejection of the host/toolchain equivalence and any waiver expiry.
owner_only_decisions: Owner accepts or rejects the reference toolchain and clean-host equivalence policy.
Owner approves any time-bounded host or toolchain waiver.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Capture the exact compiler, SDK, linker, Rust, Cargo, Git, shell, cache, target, and source-tree facts in the TASK-000002 run record before any PB-008 or PB-009 promotion.
prohibited_claims: No broad build, release confidence, production, or Chrome-class claim from reference-host evidence alone.

information_classes.name: First-entry documentation and stop/resume map
required_information: One current information path from root discovery to gate truth, task authority, research lanes, validation, and unsupported claims.
A checked start-state record that separates no-claim docs and research from proposed task execution and broad product work.
missing_information: Owner-reviewed build-readiness closure beyond no-claim templates before any all-information-ready-for-building or broad build claim.
Accepted task-specific manifests for proposed queue work before any proposed task executes.
owner_only_decisions: Owner accepts or rejects documentation completion beyond contained M0.
Owner promotes any start-state or closure-review record from no-claim inventory to build authorization.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Keep entrypoint records synchronized whenever a lane gains evidence, a validator changes, or a task status changes.

information_classes.name: Task authority and evidence-bundle readiness
required_information: Reviewed immutable task manifests with owner, independent reviewer, allowed paths, prohibited paths, budgets, rollback, expiry, and evidence-bundle location.
Accepted or rejected evidence bundles produced by independent review for execution tasks.
missing_information: Owner-approved immutable manifests for proposed TASK-000001 through TASK-000010.
Independent accepted or rejected evidence bundle for TASK-000011.
owner_only_decisions: Owner approves any proposed task for execution.
Owner accepts TASK-000011 or rejects it after independent review.
Owner promotes any task to release-gated status.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Prepare one task-specific approval record only after the owner picks a proposed task and reviewer.

information_classes.name: Source strategy and ADR-0009
required_information: Owner-reviewed ADR-0009 source decision covering selected baseline, source provenance, generated output, license, SBOM, unsafe, FFI, component boundaries, compatibility, performance, security, maintenance, and public-claim impact.
Evidence for every ADR9-EV-001 through ADR9-EV-018 item or an explicit retained blocker.
missing_information: Selected source baseline and feature profile with owner-reviewed provenance and equivalence policy.
Owner-reviewed generated-output, unsafe, FFI, license, SBOM, local compatibility, performance, security, and maintenance decisions.
owner_only_decisions: Owner accepts or rejects Servo or any other source baseline.
Owner authorizes any browser-engine source in a release path.
Owner approves component boundaries and JavaScript-runtime conflict handling.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Continue TASK-000001 as no-claim source-strategy evidence until an owner starts a real ADR-0009 decision review.

information_classes.name: Fresh-host build reproduction
required_information: Independent fresh-host or owner-approved clean-VM bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask logs with exact OS, shell, Rust, Cargo, Git, cache, target directory, temp directory, and source-tree cleanliness.
Owner-reviewed fresh-host readiness beyond the checked no-claim readiness-review template.
missing_information: Independent retained run records with output hashes and failure denominator.
Owner-reviewed fresh-host readiness decision beyond the no-claim template.
owner_only_decisions: Owner designates the reference host or approves clean-VM equivalence.
Owner promotes PB-009 from partial to ready.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Convert TASK-000002 into a reviewed manifest only when the fresh reference host or clean-VM scope is known.

information_classes.name: Process authority and IPC information
required_information: Accepted TASK-000011 independent evidence and owner-reviewed IPC readiness beyond the no-claim template.

Canonical wire encoding, authenticated transport, handle transfer, stale-epoch receiver behavior, timeout and cancellation behavior, and negative coverage for malformed, oversized, stale, duplicate, reordered, unauthorized, and wrong-principal messages.
missing_information: Accepted or rejected independent TASK-000011 evidence bundle.
Owner-reviewed IPC readiness and platform transport evidence beyond current M0 reference tests.
owner_only_decisions: Owner accepts TASK-000011 and PB-011 readiness.
Owner approves wire format, transport authority model, schema-source approval, and production process topology.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Get independent review of TASK-000011, then scope TASK-000003 negative tests and transport design from the IPC templates.

information_classes.name: Sandbox probe and containment information
required_information: Executable expected-deny probe packages for renderer, network, storage, GPU, decoder, extension, DevTools, agent, and updater roles across filesystem, socket, process, registry, device, shared-memory, credential, debug, profile, and IPC access.
Effective platform-policy capture, unsandboxed controls, broker fixtures, compromised-client harnesses, platform matrix evidence, and owner-reviewed sandbox readiness.
missing_information: Packaged probe execution records beyond the no-claim template.
Owner-reviewed platform sandbox policy and readiness decision.
owner_only_decisions: Owner accepts platform sandbox policy and waiver scope.
Owner promotes sandbox readiness, SEC-GATE-1, SEC-GATE-6, or site-isolation evidence.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Turn the WP-003 operation catalog into task-scoped probe packages only after IPC authority scope is accepted or explicitly isolated.

information_classes.name: Benchmark, Chrome-class performance, and raw-result information
required_information: Fixed hardware, OS controls, browser pins, local corpus, network profiles, launch runner, trace/artifact package, 30-tab scenario, raw result, statistics analysis, claim bundle, and owner-reviewed benchmark readiness evidence.
Equal-workload competitor runs with disclosed site-isolation, tab lifecycle, update, profile, cache, extension, and discard settings before any Chrome-class or performance claim.
missing_information: Browser-run raw artifacts, trace packages, memory samples, energy samples, and failure records.
Owner-reviewed statistics analysis and claim bundles beyond no-claim templates.
owner_only_decisions: Owner approves fixed benchmark hardware tiers, browser pins, statistics-analysis plan scope, claim-bundle publication, and benchmark readiness.
Owner authorizes any public speed, memory, energy, or Chrome-class comparison.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Continue no-claim TASK-000005 runner and artifact-package preparation without producing benchmark claims.

information_classes.name: Native shell, page surface, component, and accessibility information
required_information: Accepted ADR-0013, ADR-0014, and ADR-0016 decisions, equivalent reference-shell adapter evidence, toolkit-neutral state/command/surface/accessibility/diagnostic contracts, and no-toolkit-owned-authority negative tests.

Rendered component fixtures, UI-GATE-7 and UI-GATE-10 evidence, page-tree composition proof, IME and keyboard proof, manual assistive-technology coverage, crash, renderer-hang, GPU-loss, startup, memory, binary, latency, frame-pacing, energy, license, dependency, provenance, and package/runtime-exclusion evidence.
missing_information: Equivalent adapter prototypes and rendered fixture evidence beyond current no-claim inventories.
Owner-reviewed native UI readiness, toolkit selection, page-surface approval, and accessibility readiness.
owner_only_decisions: Owner accepts ADR-0013, ADR-0014, ADR-0016, UI-GATE-7, UI-GATE-10, toolkit selection, and compositor ownership.
Owner approves trusted-chrome, accessibility, page-surface, page-tree, or release-path UI readiness.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Prepare task-scoped native-shell bake-off manifests only after IPC command authority is accepted or the shell prototype is explicitly isolated.

information_classes.name: Reference desktop platform support and scope information
required_information: Owner-scoped reference platform, architecture, minimum OS/image, compositor/windowing environment, hardware tier, and support boundary.
Executable native window, display, input, IME, accessibility, page-surface, graphics, sandbox, packaging, recovery, and diagnostic workflows, plus clean-host and fixed-hardware evidence.
missing_information: Platform selection and owner-approved minimum support scope beyond the no-claim scorecard.
Reference-platform execution, clean-host reproduction, native workflow, benchmark/resource, packaging/recovery, incident-capacity, and support-owner evidence.
owner_only_decisions: Owner selects the reference platform and accepts its support, unsupported, maintenance, incident, and release boundaries.
Owner approves any supported-platform, compatibility, accessibility, security, performance, release, or production claim.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Keep PB-006 not_selected and run the scorecard only after the owner defines the M1 product-support scope and an approved platform evidence task.
prohibited_claims: No compatibility, accessibility, security, performance, release, or production claim.

information_classes.name: Profile, Space, session, snapshot, and migration information
required_information: Executable versioned profile, Space, session, snapshot, and migration schemas.

Disk-full, power-loss, corruption, downgrade, export, deletion, private-session, crash-recovery, protected-work, privacy, data-loss, real-profile fixture, sync, credential-storage, and user-data handling evidence.
missing_information: Executable schema proposals and fault/migration tests beyond the schema-package template.
Owner-reviewed profile/session readiness and real-profile fixture policy.
owner_only_decisions: Owner approves profile/session readiness, data-loss safety, real-profile migration, sync, credential storage, and release-path profile behavior.
Owner promotes any production profile-format claim.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Draft task-specific schema-package evidence only after the owner confirms no real user profile data is in scope.

information_classes.name: Research package, update, rollback, and signing-boundary information
required_information: Executable signed research-package manifest, metadata parser, fake-key local signature verification, staged install, rollback, migration, downgrade, crash-loop, tamper, replay, wrong-target, partial-write, disk-full, and power-loss evidence.
Explicit production-key separation and owner-reviewed package/update readiness before any updater or release claim.
missing_information: Executable package/update lab output beyond no-claim templates.
Owner-reviewed package/update readiness, production-key separation review, supported-security review, and release-readiness approval.
owner_only_decisions: Owner approves package/update readiness, signing readiness, stable channel, public distribution, supported security, and production updater boundaries.
Owner authorizes any use of production signing or offline root material.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Keep TASK-000009 isolated to fake-key local research-package evidence until release authority exists.

information_classes.name: Security incident and emergency patch information
required_information: Executed private-intake tabletop, access control, acknowledgement, reproduction, severity, asset analysis, embargo handling, protected patch branch, embargoed CI, regression, backport, signing/update dry run, staged rollout, minimum secure version, revocation, release notes, CVE/credit handling, coordinated disclosure, and postmortem evidence.
Role matrix, escalation, timing, secret rotation, backup coverage, no-agent-authority controls, and owner-reviewed incident/patch readiness.
missing_information: Executed tabletop and emergency patch dry-run records beyond no-claim templates.
Owner-reviewed incident/patch readiness and demonstrated update capacity.
owner_only_decisions: Owner decides severity, disclosure, stable promotion, signing authority, supported security versions, and incident closure.
Owner approves production-safe browsing or supported-security claims.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Keep TASK-000010 as a private tabletop and authority-separation exercise until backup owners and update lab evidence exist.

information_classes.name: Backup ownership and two-person control information
required_information: Named qualified backup owners for build-critical program, architecture, security, release-operations, human-release-authority, incident-response, legal-community, support, quality, supply-chain, documentation-research, product, platform, engine, JavaScript, networking, storage, performance, accessibility, UI-runtime, agent-operations, and privacy-data scopes.

Role-level evidence, subsystem competence, representative path coverage, recent review record, availability, succession, recusal, inactivity, removal, emergency replacement, CODEOWNERS and review-rule reconciliation, no stale privileged access, no ownerless protected paths, and two-person control evidence.
missing_information: Named qualified backups and qualification records beyond the no-claim template.
Owner-reviewed backup ownership readiness and two-person control evidence.
owner_only_decisions: Owner names and verifies qualified backup owners.
Owner approves owner coverage, two-person control, release authority, signing authority, disclosure authority, legal approval, incident closure, and production authority.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Keep PB-019 blocked until real owner identities and qualification evidence are available.

information_classes.name: Chrome-class product and capability information
required_information: Traceability from Chrome-class product expectations to capability parity, product requirements, security, accessibility, performance, DevTools, extension, enterprise, update, sync, media, printing, PDF, AI, and operating evidence.
Owner-reviewed capability evidence and claim bundles for every public comparison axis before using Chrome-class or number-one language.
missing_information: Accepted capability evidence for each Chrome-class domain, not only routing and requirements.
Owner-reviewed public-claim bundles covering compatibility, security, accessibility, performance, memory, energy, stability, update, incident-response, and support obligations.
owner_only_decisions: Owner authorizes any Chrome-class, Chrome-equivalent, number-one, daily-driver, or public comparison statement.
Owner promotes a capability area from target or requirement to supported behavior.
information_classes.next_research_or_review: Use the Chrome-class traceability map to choose the next evidence lane without making capability claims.
